identitySentence: The Law Office of Kimberly Shanklin, PLLC is a Denton solo practice focused exclusively on Denton County family law and criminal law, led by an attorney who has practiced since 2002.

concept.headline: When family or freedom is on the line in Denton County, talk with attorney Kimberly Shanklin.
concept.subheadline: Facing a divorce, a custody dispute, or a criminal charge is overwhelming, and the hardest step is asking for help. At the Law Office of Kimberly Shanklin, PLLC, your inquiry goes straight to a solo attorney who has practiced law since 2002 and works exclusively on Denton County cases.
credibilityPoints: Lead Counsel Verified in Criminal Defense, Divorce, and DUI since 2021
Voted Best Criminal Law Office in Denton County, with Expertise.com Best of Denton County awards 2015 through 2020
Handles family law and criminal law cases exclusively in Denton County

sections.title: Practice focus
sections.body: The firm handles family law and criminal law matters, and only in Denton County. On the criminal defense side, that includes felonies, misdemeanors, theft, assault, drug possession, expungements, and non-disclosures. Founding attorney Kimberly Shanklin spent 13 years at a larger firm before building her solo practice.

sections.title: Denton roots
sections.body: The Law Office of Kimberly Shanklin, PLLC is located at 1517 Centre Place Drive in Denton and works exclusively on cases in Denton County. This is a practice built for one community, not a region.

sections.title: What to expect
sections.body: The office works by appointment only, so every consultation request starts with a few honest questions: your name, how to reach you, the county your matter is in, and a short description of your situation. From there, the firm follows up directly to schedule a time to talk.

outreachDraft.subject: Proof matters more than promises: 15 days, 1 demo, and a Lead Capturer that can pay for itself before you ever have to.
outreachDraft.body:
DRAFT — NOT SENT. Review and authorize before any outreach.

To the Law Office of Kimberly Shanklin, PLLC:

Your firm states plainly that it works exclusively on Denton County family law and criminal law cases. That kind of focus is rare, and it is exactly why we built something with you in mind.

Your website already does many things well: a clear Request a Consultation call to action, genuine client testimonials, and strong Denton County positioning. What it cannot do today is answer a potential client the moment they reach out. Consultation requests arrive through a form or a phone call during office hours, and because your office works by appointment only, an inquiry sent at 9 p.m. waits until someone can reply. For a solo practitioner balancing court dates and client work, those waiting inquiries are often the ones that quietly call the next firm on the list.

The Lead Capturer closes that gap. It is a custom intake page built for your firm, carrying your own identity sentence: "The Law Office of Kimberly Shanklin, PLLC is a Denton solo practice focused exclusively on Denton County family law and criminal law, led by an attorney who has practiced since 2002." Its contact form is written for your practice, so a potential client is asked the questions that actually matter for a Denton County family law or criminal law matter.

Here is how it works:

1. Every inquiry is emailed directly to your firm's inbox.
2. The potential client automatically receives a topical response specific to their message, whether they wrote about a divorce, a custody dispute, or a criminal charge.
3. That response presents your available consultation times.
4. A $50 reservation deposit confirms the appointment, so the people on your calendar have committed before you spend a minute preparing.

We would rather you prove it to yourself than take our word for anything. We are offering a 15-day proof period: test the complete experience, end to end, exactly as a potential client would. If it does not work precisely as described, you owe nothing.

How to test your Lead Capturer (15 minutes):

1. Submit a clearly labeled TEST inquiry using a customer email address you control.
2. Do not enter confidential client information at any point.
3. Confirm the firm inbox receives a real email.
4. Confirm the notification contains every displayed form field.
5. Confirm the subject line identifies your firm and the likely matter type.
6. Confirm the Reply-To address contains the submitted customer email.
7. Confirm the system automatically generates a response specific to the TEST inquiry.
8. Confirm the response is sent to the customer email with no action from your staff.
9. Confirm the customer email actually receives it.
10. Confirm it addresses the actual topic of the inquiry.
11. Confirm genuine available consultation times are presented.
12. Select a time.
13. Confirm the $50 reservation requirement appears.
14. Confirm the preparation list fits the matter.
15. Confirm the prohibited-item list includes legal documents.
16. In a payment sandbox, confirm the appointment locks only after a verified $50 payment event.

If any step fails, the test fails, and we want to hear about it. Contact us and we will make it right or part as neighbors.

Respectfully,

The IntakeBrief Team
[Physical mailing address: provided before any send]

If you would prefer not to hear from us again, reply with 'unsubscribe' and we will remove you immediately.
